implementations

Class MyQueue<E>

  • java.lang.Object
    • implementations.MyQueue<E>
  • Type Parameters:
    E - The type of elements held in this queue.
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    QueueADT<E>

    public class MyQueue<E>
extends java.lang.Object
implements QueueADT<E>
    MyQueue is a MyDLL-based implementation of the QueueADT interface. It follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle: elements are enqueued at the tail of the underlying list and dequeued from the head.

    This implementation does not use any classes from the java.util.* collection framework, other than NoSuchElementException which is a standard Java exception used by the iterator.

    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Vansh

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      MyQueue()
      Constructs an empty MyQueue.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      boolean contains(E toFind)
      Returns true if this queue contains the specified element.
      E dequeue()
      Removes and returns the element at the front of this queue.
      void dequeueAll()
      Removes all items in this queue.
      void enqueue(E toAdd)
      Places the specified element at the back of this queue.
      boolean equals(QueueADT<E> that)
      Compares the specified QueueADT with this queue for equality.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true when this queue contains no items.
      boolean isFull()
      Returns true if the queue is at capacity.
      Iterator<E> iterator()
      Returns an iterator over the elements in this queue, from front to back, in proper sequence.
      E peek()
      Returns the element at the front of this queue without removing it.
      int search(E toFind)
      Returns the 1-based position from the front of this queue where the specified element is located.
      int size()
      Returns the length of the current queue as an integer value.
      java.lang.Object[] toArray()
      Returns an Object array containing all elements in this queue in order from front to back.
      E[] toArray(E[] holder)
      Returns an array containing all elements in this queue in order from front to back.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • MyQueue

        public MyQueue()
        Constructs an empty MyQueue.

    • Method Detail

      • enqueue

        public void enqueue(E toAdd)
             throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Places the specified element at the back of this queue.

        Pre-condition: toAdd must not be null.

        Post-condition: The element is appended to the back of the queue and the queue size increases by one.

        Specified by:
        enqueue in interface QueueADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toAdd - The item to be added to the queue.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified element is null.

      • dequeue

        public E dequeue()
          throws EmptyQueueException
        Removes and returns the element at the front of this queue.

        Pre-condition: The queue must not be empty.

        Post-condition: The front element is removed and the queue size decreases by one.

        Specified by:
        dequeue in interface QueueADT<E>
        Returns:
        The first item in the queue.
        Throws:
        EmptyQueueException - If the queue's length is zero.

      • peek

        public E peek()
       throws EmptyQueueException
        Returns the element at the front of this queue without removing it.

        Pre-condition: The queue must not be empty.

        Post-condition: The queue remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        peek in interface QueueADT<E>
        Returns:
        The first item in the queue.
        Throws:
        EmptyQueueException - If the queue's length is zero.

      • dequeueAll

        public void dequeueAll()
        Removes all items in this queue.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The queue is empty and its size is zero.

        Specified by:
        dequeueAll in interface QueueADT<E>

      • isEmpty

        public boolean isEmpty()
        Returns true when this queue contains no items.
        Specified by:
        isEmpty in interface QueueADT<E>
        Returns:
        true when the queue length is zero.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(E toFind)
                 throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Returns true if this queue contains the specified element.

        Pre-condition: toFind must not be null.

        Post-condition: The queue remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        contains in interface QueueADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toFind - The element whose presence is to be tested.
        Returns:
        true if the queue contains the element.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified element is null.

      • search

        public int search(E toFind)
        Returns the 1-based position from the front of this queue where the specified element is located. The first item on the queue is at distance 1. Returns -1 if the object is not found.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The queue remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        search in interface QueueADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toFind - The desired object.
        Returns:
        The 1-based position from the front of the queue, or -1 if not found.

      • iterator

        public Iterator<E> iterator()
        Returns an iterator over the elements in this queue, from front to back, in proper sequence.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: The queue remains unchanged.

        Specified by:
        iterator in interface QueueADT<E>
        Returns:
        An Iterator over the elements in this queue.

      • equals

        public boolean equals(QueueADT<E> that)
        Compares the specified QueueADT with this queue for equality. Two queues are equal if they have the same size and contain equal items appearing in the same order.

        Pre-condition: None.

        Post-condition: Both queues remain unchanged.

        Specified by:
        equals in interface QueueADT<E>
        Parameters:
        that - The queue to compare with this queue.
        Returns:
        true if the queues are equal.

      • toArray

        public java.lang.Object[] toArray()
        Returns an Object array containing all elements in this queue in order from front to back.
        Specified by:
        toArray in interface QueueADT<E>
        Returns:
        An array containing all elements of the queue, front to back.

      • toArray

        public E[] toArray(E[] holder)
            throws java.lang.NullPointerException
        Returns an array containing all elements in this queue in order from front to back. If the given array is large enough it is used; otherwise a new array of the same runtime type is allocated.
        Specified by:
        toArray in interface QueueADT<E>
        Parameters:
        toHold - The array into which the elements of this queue are to be stored, if it is big enough.
        Returns:
        An array containing all queue elements from front to back.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If the specified array is null.

      • isFull

        public boolean isFull()
        Returns true if the queue is at capacity. Since this is a dynamically sized queue with no fixed upper bound, this method always returns false.
        Specified by:
        isFull in interface QueueADT<E>
        Returns:
        false always, as this queue has no fixed capacity.

      • size

        public int size()
        Returns the length of the current queue as an integer value.
        Specified by:
        size in interface QueueADT<E>
        Returns:
        The current size of the queue.
